hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, multi-stage MD/GCMC simulations, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the chemical space of linear polyamines, then it will efficiently identify high-performance CO2 adsorbents with superior adsorption capacity compared to known reference polyamines within a limited simulation budget, because the surrogate model-guided search can exploit structural patterns encoded in polymer-specific embeddings to prioritize promising candidates without exhaustive enumeration.
